# MAIAS v0.2 — condensed reference

MAIAS (Mobile App Information Architecture Schema) describes an app's information
architecture — screens, navigation, flows, UI elements, states, data — in one YAML
(or JSON) document. This file is sufficient to author a valid document; the formal
definition is schema/maias.schema.json; full spec: docs/spec/.

Validate with: maias validate <file>   Format with: maias fmt <file> --write

## Document skeleton

maias: "0.2"                    # required; quoted string, never a number. Versions: "0.1" or
                                # "0.2" (current). back: is 0.2-only — declare "0.2" to use it;
                                # a document without 0.2 features may declare "0.1".
app:
  name: Todo List              # required
  description: A minimal list-keeping app.   # recommended
  links:                       # optional — deep-link scheme
    scheme: todo
  navigation:                  # required — the registry
    primary:
      label: Primary Nav
      screens:                 # tab bar, in tab order (renderers hide the bar if <2)
        - home
    secondary:
      label: Secondary Nav
      screens:                 # EVERY other screen must be listed here
        - task_detail
  flows:                       # required, >=1; first flow = app start
    - name: main               # snake_case, unique
      description: Browse and manage tasks.
      entry_screen: home       # must exist AND be listed in this flow's screens
      screens:
        - home
        - task_detail
screens:                       # required, >=1
  - id: home                   # snake_case ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$, unique
    title: My Tasks            # required, Title Case
    type: list                 # required, see screen types
    path: /tasks               # required, unique, kebab-case, leading /
    description: All tasks, newest first.   # lint warns if missing
    features:                  # optional documentation-level capability names
      - task_list
    actions:                   # optional documentation-level action NAMES (snake_case strings,
      - add_task               #   like features). The rendered buttons live in navigation.actions.
    elements:                  # optional UI declarations, render order (see taxonomy)
      - label: Search tasks
        type: search_bar
      - label: Buy milk
        type: list_item
        target: task_detail    # any target must be an existing screen id
    states:                    # optional; keys limited to empty/loading/error
      empty:
        description: First run, or all tasks deleted.
        elements:              # FULL replacement list, not a merge
          - label: No tasks yet
            type: empty_state
    navigation:
      # primary:               # allowed ONLY on primary (tab-bar) screens; redundant with the
      #   - label: My Tasks    #   registry by design and must match app.navigation.primary
      #     target: home       #   (lint warns otherwise). Omitting it everywhere is fine.
      secondary:               # contextual links
        - label: Task Detail
          target: task_detail
          presentation: modal  # optional override: push|replace|modal|sheet
      actions:                 # non-navigating (external: true = leaves the app or hands
                               #   off to the system: web links, share sheets, mail composers)
        - label: Rate this app
          external: true
    data:
      reads:                   # snake_case domain data keys
        - tasks
      writes:
        - task_status
  - id: task_detail
    title: Task Detail
    type: detail
    path: /tasks/:id           # :param binds from URL
    description: One task, editable status.
    presentation: modal        # optional screen default: push(default)|replace|modal|sheet
    auth: required             # optional: none(default)|required
    deep_link: true            # optional, default false; needs app.links.scheme
    back:                      # optional (since 0.2): the header back button. No back key = no button.
      target: home             #   required, existing screen id (dangling = error)
      # label: Back            #   optional; default label = target screen's title

## Screen types (semantic; do not restrict elements)

action        single-purpose action trigger
action_sheet  bottom sheet with choices (should have presentation: sheet)
container     parent grouping child screens
detail        read-only detail view
form          user input / editable fields
informational static content / instructions
list          scrollable list of items
map           map-based interface
marketing     promotional / value proposition
menu          links to sub-screens
search        search input with results
status        live status display
Custom screen types MUST be x_ prefixed (x_vendor_name); anything else unknown = schema error.

## Element taxonomy (29 core types)

Element = {type required; label per-type; target optional (must exist); presentation optional with target}.
Label formats: "|"-separated = multi-value (whitespace around | is trimmed, "A | B" == "A|B");
"\n"-separated = multi-line; literal none = a DELIBERATE skeleton placeholder. Only type is
schema-required: for text-free types (image, divider, video) write label: "" or omit the key.
CAUTION: write \n-bearing labels as DOUBLE-QUOTED scalars ("line1\nline2") — an unquoted
multi-line YAML scalar folds newlines into spaces and silently merges your lines.

TEXT
title        hero heading; label required
heading      section heading; label required
paragraph    body text; label required
caption      small centred muted text (dividers "or", legal); label required
bullets      bulleted list; label = \n-separated lines, or none for skeleton bars
label_value  key-value row; label = "Key | Value"
LAYOUT
divider      horizontal rule; label ignored
spacer       vertical gap; label = height px as string (default 16)
card         bordered container with bold title; label required; target optional
ACTIONS
button       full-width CTA; label required; target optional
icon_button  circular icon + label below; label required; target optional
link         inline underlined text; label required; target optional
chips        pill row (tags/filters); label = "A | B | C"
INPUTS
text_field   input box; label = placeholder
search_bar   pill search input; label = placeholder (default "Search")
toggle       label + switch row; label required
checkbox     checkbox + label row; label required
radio_group  single-choice options; label = "A | B | C"
dropdown     select row with chevron; label required
slider       value slider; label required
MEDIA
image        16:9 image block; label ignored
avatar       circle with initial from label (default "U")
map          4:3 map region; label ignored
video        16:9 video block; label ignored
LISTS
list_item    tappable row, chevron when target set; label required; target optional
empty_state  centred "nothing here" message; label required
FEEDBACK
banner       full-width notice strip; label required
progress     progress bar, label above; label required
segmented_control  in-screen tab row; label = "Tab1 | Tab2" (NOT the app tab bar)

Unknown element types: custom types MUST be x_ prefixed (x_vendor_name).
Renderers MUST render unknown types via fallback and MUST NOT fail.
Lint warns on unprefixed unknown types (probable typo); never an error.

## Extension fields

Any object may carry x_-prefixed extra fields (x_experiment: paywall_v2).
Consumers ignore unknown x_ fields and preserve them through edits.
Unknown fields WITHOUT x_ prefix are schema errors (catches typos).

## Chrome

Tab bar := app.navigation.primary.screens (label = screen title) — derived, never declared;
no tab_bar/header elements. Headers show the title automatically. The BACK button is DECLARED
via the screen's back key (see skeleton): give every non-tab, non-entry screen a back target;
tab roots and journey entries (any flow's entry_screen) omit it. Never model back as a
navigation.secondary item.
Modal/sheet screens may declare back too — it names the explicit dismiss target. A screen
reached from several places declares its DOMINANT entry as back.target; renderers SHOULD
honour real history when available (spec §4.3), so the static target is the cold-start answer.

## Presentation modes

push (default) advance in journey; replace = journey transition (onboarding→main, no back);
modal = focused overlay; sheet = bottom sheet. Resolution: link presentation > screen
presentation > push.

## Hard rules (validator errors)

- ids unique; paths unique and non-colliding (/t/:a vs /t/:b collides). A static segment
  and a :param at the same position do NOT collide — the static route wins (/tasks/new
  alongside /tasks/:id is legal and resolves to /tasks/new for that URL)
- every navigation/element/back target is an existing screen id (no dangling targets)
- every screen in exactly ONE of app.navigation primary.screens/secondary.screens
- flow entry_screen exists and is in the flow's screens; flow names unique
- id/flow/param: ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$; path: ^/[a-z0-9-]+(/([a-z0-9-]+|:[a-z][a-z0-9_]*))*$

## Warnings (valid but flagged)

- screen missing description; unreachable screens; orphans (no flow + unreachable)
- self-referencing target; unprefixed unknown element type
- deep_link without app.links.scheme; navigation.primary on non-primary screens
- 0.2-only keys (back:) under maias: "0.1" — declare "0.2"
- action_sheet screens without presentation: sheet

## Canonical form (maias fmt; SHOULD for documents, editors emit on write)

- Key order — document: maias, app, screens. app: name, description, links, navigation,
  flows. screen: id, title, type, path, description, presentation, auth, deep_link,
  back, features, actions, elements, states, navigation, data. items: label, type,
  target, presentation, external (in that order, when present); back: label, target.
  x_ keys last.
- Omit empty/default keys (no "actions: - None", no deep_link: false).
- Screens ordered by flow membership (flow order, then within-flow order; multi-flow
  screens under first flow; flowless screens last, sorted by id).
- 2-space indent; block style only (never [] or {}); plain scalars; maias header quoted.
- No YAML anchors/tags/merge keys. Comments allowed, non-semantic, MUST survive edits;
  put intent in description fields, not comments.

## Authoring guidance

- Intent lives in description fields (app, screens, flows, states) — structure says what,
  descriptions say why.
- Nest paths to mirror navigation (children under their hub's path).
- 5-15 elements per screen; declare repeated rows once (one list_item = the row type).
- Deep-linked screens should be renderable from path params + data.reads alone.
- data keys name domain data (cart_items), not implementation (api_response);
  same key = same data across screens.
